title: Köplägen i hälsovårdssektorn – hör förvaltaren
summary_md:
# Köplägen i hälsovårdssektorn – hör förvaltaren

**Feed**: dagen-med-di  
**Published**: 2026-06-06  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A healthcare-focused episode featuring Healthinvest portfolio manager Astrid Samuelsson, discussing how the healthcare sector has lagged the broad market (up 9% vs 27% over a year) and now trades at a valuation gap (forward P/E ~17 vs ~20 for the broad market). Large-cap medtech has been the weakest sub-sector while biotech and smaller pharma/drug-development names have thrived, creating a guldålder for developing new drugs. The standout theme is the diverging fortunes of obesity giants Eli Lilly (strong growth, ~30% topline, beating estimates) versus Novo Nordisk (negative topline growth, profit warnings, looming patent expiry).

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 LLY — Eli Lilly | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Strongest player in obesity market, guiding ~30% growth, Q1 beat estimates by $2bn, superior pipeline, patent and financial position.

#### Reasoning

The manager highlights Lilly as the clearly stronger obesity franchise versus Novo: better product portfolio, pipeline, patent situation and financials. Q1 alone beat estimates by $2bn on obesity demand and the company guides ~30% topline growth. A higher multiple than Novo is judged reasonable. No explicit recommendation is given (manager declines), but the directional view is strongly positive.

#### Sell Triggers

- if obesity demand slows materially
- if pipeline/clinical setbacks emerge
- if Novo closes the competitive gap

> *"Lilly slog estimat med två miljarder dollar på grund av obesitas."*

---

### ⛔ NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Negative topline growth guided this year, repeated profit warnings, estimates cut sharply, and key obesity patent expiring in 4-5 years.

#### Reasoning

While still a leader in obesity, Novo is described as facing negative topline growth, a string of profit warnings and materially lowered estimates. A looming patent expiry on its key obesity drug (4-5 years out) justifies a lower multiple than Lilly. The divergence versus Lilly has widened and continues to deteriorate. Directionally negative, though no formal recommendation is issued.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth guidance turns positive
- if pipeline delivers new obesity catalysts
- if estimate cuts stabilise

> *"istället Novo ser framför sig en negativ tillväxt på topline i år"*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **LLY** | Eli Lilly | 🟢 BUY | MT | 65% | Strongest player in obesity market, guiding ~30% growth, Q1 beat estimates by $2bn, superior pipeline, patent and financial position. |
| **NOVO-B** | Novo Nordisk | ⛔ AVOID | MT | 60% | Negative topline growth guided this year, repeated profit warnings, estimates cut sharply, and key obesity patent expiring in 4-5 years. |
